207008.explanation: The costs of school, bicycles and laptops are unaffordable for some families. We want children from families with less money to be able to participate just like everyone else. That is why we think it is fair for the local authority to pay for this.

207009.explanation: In many streets, everything revolves around the car. We can counter this by making the car a guest. Cars must then adapt to children playing and pedestrians. Cars are welcome, but life on the street is more important.

207010.explanation: The space on the street belongs to everyone. Parking takes up a lot of space, leaving less room for children, bicycles and greenery. Fewer parking spaces and more space for playing, cycling and trees will create a pleasant municipality for everyone.

207014.explanation: Car emissions cause air pollution. The environmental zone ensures that fewer commercial vehicles drive in the city centre. This makes the air in the city cleaner and healthier. Clean air is vital for people.

207016.explanation: Sex work is work. Groningen helps anyone seeking a new job so we also help sex workers. If sex workers are being exploited, it is important that the municipality helps them escape this situation by, among other things, providing accessible programmes with assistance.

206950.explanation: People now get lost in a bureaucratic maze. We want one team and one contact point for damage, reinforcement and compensation: personal, clear, and only done when it’s truly solved.

206952.explanation: Students deserve good support, but not a separate department. We want clear, well-functioning services for everyone: less bureaucracy, more solutions—for students and other Groningers.

206954.explanation: Heritage and local traditions deserve support, but not at the expense of other culture. We choose a broad cultural policy with room for neighbourhood culture, amateur arts and young makers.

206957.explanation: You shouldn’t pit care against climate. Good climate policy lowers energy bills and is anti-poverty policy. But if national cuts force choices, we will always protect care. People come before projects.

206958.explanation: An absolute ban is unworkable: the city could hardly do business with anyone. It would hit small entrepreneurs without alternatives. We do break with big fossil multinationals and invest in public, clean energy. Tackle the polluter, not the market vendor.

206959.explanation: Terraces use public space to earn money, so a fair fee is logical. It helps maintain public space and prevents costs being shifted to residents who don’t profit from it.

206960.explanation: We spare small businesses and social organisations. We do tax large advertising from big companies—they profit from our public space and should contribute fairly. That keeps the city pleasant and shares the burden fairly.

206965.explanation: Stopping shelter for people without a valid permit helps no one. They may not stay but cannot leave yet. Without shelter they disappear onto the streets, causing more unrest and nuisance. Bed-bath-bread keeps them in view and under supervision.

206966.explanation: Living with your boss makes workers dependent and too often leads to abuse. We demand decent housing and enforce strictly, so labour migrants live safely and neighbourhoods don’t pay the price.

206967.explanation: The Stadjerspas is for residents on a low income, not for specific groups. Refugees can get it once they live here and meet the income rules. That keeps it fair, simple and equal for everyone.

206973.explanation: Keeping Warmtestad public lets the city steer for affordable tariffs instead of profit. We want heat to become cheaper and fairer for Groningers, not more expensive.

206975.explanation: More forest and green space means cooling, cleaner air and nice places to relax. We invest in new woodland where Groningers truly benefit, with attention to nature and accessibility.

206976.explanation: Events add to the city, but people must also be able to live and sleep. Clear limits on times and noise protect residents and keep Groningen liveable, while leaving room for fun.

206980.explanation: Drug testing prevents accidents and saves lives. Nightlife areas need accessible places where people can test safely. That’s not encouragement, but sensible public health policy that reduces harm.

206981.explanation: Every child should be able to play sports, regardless of parents’ income. Sport boosts health, confidence and having fun together. That’s why we ensure fees are never a barrier for children.

206986.explanation: Every child should experience art and culture, regardless of income at home. It boosts creativity, confidence and joy. Investing in this gives children more chances to develop.

206987.explanation: We’ve seen at clubs like Vitesse how risky dependence on commercial investors can be. With supporters owning half, FC Groningen stays in Groningers’ hands. A football club is not a toy for investors.

206988.explanation: We support a good pop venue near the station, but ‘big’ isn’t a goal in itself. It must fit demand and stay within budget. No prestige project, but a sober, smart investment that gives Groningen a strong pop stage.

206989.explanation: BOAs are not the police. Arming them raises the risk of escalation. We improve safety by investing in neighbourhoods: more community and youth workers and strong cooperation with local police. Prevent problems instead of letting them escalate.

206993.explanation: New-build housing should first benefit people who live, work or study in Groningen. A local-connection rule helps keep homes from ending up with investors and keeps neighbourhoods liveable for Groningers.

206994.explanation: Teachers, nurses and police officers are essential for our city. If high housing costs or part-time work block an affordable home, we should ensure they can still live here. That keeps Groningen running.

206996.explanation: Homes are for living, not for profit. Investors and slumlords drive up prices and make housing unaffordable. An owner-occupancy requirement keeps homes for people who want to live here.

206998.explanation: Without design rules, building quickly becomes a mess. Clear standards ensure new homes fit the neighbourhood and keep Groningen beautiful and liveable.

207002.explanation: Affordable rent should be possible in every neighbourhood and village. Social housing shouldn’t be concentrated in just a few areas. We build where it’s missing to break the divide and keep Groningen one municipality.

207003.explanation: If someone does socially useful work, they deserve proper pay. Mandatory ‘return’ work isn’t a solution but disguised forced labour. We want real jobs with prospects, not unpaid work.

207008.explanation: For other types of fraud there are no separate click-lines. A special reporting tool stigmatizes people on benefits and fuels mistrust. We choose equality and trust, with normal checks like for everyone else.

207009.explanation: In some streets cars should be ‘guests’, giving more space to pedestrians, cyclists and children at play. That makes neighbourhoods safer and more liveable. We do it where it fits, together with residents.

207010.explanation: In many areas parking is already difficult. Simply removing spaces increases pressure and just moves the problem. We keep parking workable and decide per neighbourhood what’s needed so people can park near home.

207014.explanation: 30 km/h makes streets safer and calmer, but only if roads are designed for it, with safe crossings and less rat-running. We set 30 km/h as the norm, except on the ring road and main routes.

207016.explanation: Food gardens can mean a lot for meeting each other and sustainability. Where residents want this, the city should provide space and support. But not every neighbourhood or village is suitable. We choose tailoring, not an obligation.

206950.explanation: Minder papier, minder regels, het belang van de burger staat centraal – dat is onze inzet. Als dat hiermee slaagt, zijn wij voor. Dat team moet dan wel de besluitkracht krijgen om dit aan te pakken, anders is het niet meer dan nog een extra loket.

206952.explanation: Studenten verdienen aandacht, de gemeenteraad vertegenwoordigt hen. Maar voor DURF hoeft dit niet via nog een extra afdeling bij de gemeente – DURF kiest voor vrijheid en lagere lasten. Ook voor studenten.

206954.explanation: DURF komt op voor lokale belangen. Daarom is DURF ook van mening dat cultuursubsidies moeten gaan naar lokale tradities en erfgoed.

206957.explanation: Stop met het verspillende klimaatbeleid dat burgers armer maakt. Richt je op echte lokale behoeften zoals betaalbare zorg, of beter nog: geef het geld terug aan de Groningers via belastingverlagingen.

206958.explanation: Dat heeft geen zin en het kan ook niet. Bijna de helft van de bouwkosten van het Forum zijn notabene door de NAM betaald. De Gasunie is een van de grootste werkgevers. Het UMCG is een van de grootste aargasverbruikers van de stad.

206959.explanation: DURF wil juist minder belastingen en meer vrijheid. Daarom zijn we tegen terrasbelasting.

206960.explanation: DURF wil graag minder belastingen en meer vrijheid voor burgers én ondernemers. Daarom zijn we tegen extra belastingen voor een reclameborden, gevelreclames en lichtreclames.

206965.explanation: Belangen van Groningers eerst – betaalbare huisvesting bouwen voor locals. Tegen de tijd dat we dat hebben opgelost, is hier misschien wel ruimte voor.

206966.explanation: Voor DURF is het geen taak van de gemeente om huisvesting voor buitenlandse werknemers te betalen. Lokale werknemers kunnen nu vaak niet eens een betaalbaar onderkomen vinden. Dit mogen de werkgevers zelf oplossen.

206967.explanation: DURF is voor gelijkheid. We zijn dus tegen discriminatie op basis van afkomst. De Stadjerspas is er voor de armste Groningers, ongeacht of ze vluchteling zijn of niet

206973.explanation: DURF staat voor lagere belastingen en maximale vrijheid: bedrijven die zichzelf bedruipen via vrijwillige vergoedingen? Dat krijgt bij DURF altijd voorrang boven subsidies uit onze portemonnee.

206975.explanation: Laten we eerst ruimte maken voor onze echte problemen. Ruimte voor woningen, ruimte voor Groningers. DURF wil geen boeren gaan onteigenen. Dus hoe wil je dit realiseren? We zien liever beter onderhoud en uitbreiding van de bestaande groenvoorzieningen en parken.

206976.explanation: DURF steunt festivals: ze houden de stad levendig en interessant. De organisator moet langer door kunnen gaan, maar wel in overleg met omwonenden. Misschien is compensatie voor omwonenden een oplossing.

206980.explanation: Geen belastinggeld naar drugstesten; dat kan prima door ondernemers in het uitgaansgebied geregeld worden.

206981.explanation: Belastingen moeten zo ver omlaag dat mensen altijd de sportclub voor hun kinderen kunnen betalen.

206986.explanation: We betalen al voor kunst en cultuur. Kunst en cultuur moeten van mensen zelf komen. De gemeente kan simpelweg van musea en culturele instellingen verlangen dat ze kunst en cultuur toegankelijk maken voor de jeugd in ruil voor de bestaande subsidies.

206987.explanation: Groningen heeft een langetermijnbelang in de club als cultureel erfgoed. Het is belangrijk dat de gemeente hierbij niet financieel betrokken raakt. De gemeente kan de supporters helpen met expertise en knowhow om dit in gang te zetten.

206988.explanation: Waarom moet de belastingbetaler opdraaien voor iets met een beperkte doelgroep? Er zijn ook voldoende commerciële partijen die dit zouden willen bouwen. Het enige dat de gemeente zou hoeven doen is ruimte maken voor dit soort initiatieven.

206989.explanation: Hier is de politie voor opgeleid. We kunnen niet de lat verlagen voor het geweldsmonopolie, dat is simpelweg te ingrijpend.

206993.explanation: Hoe stel je een binding vast? Dit klinkt vaag en arbitrair. Wie het langst op de lijst staat heeft het meeste recht op een woning.

206994.explanation: Dit ondermijnt het gelijkheidsbeginsel. Niemand heeft meer rechten dan de ander. Het is ook arbitrair; hoezo zouden deze voor gaan op anderen? Hebben schoonmakers, automonteurs en vuilnismannen niet net zo veel recht op een woning?

206996.explanation: Vrijheid boven regels: verbieden om huizen te kopen voor kinderen of familie? Of voor verhuur? Wat lost dit precies op? Bovendien, moet je een organisatie optuigen om bij mensen thuis te controleren. Lijkt ons onwenselijk.

206998.explanation: Ja, onder voorwaarden: versoepeling welstand voor minder rompslomp; bouwen blijft hoogwaardig en passend in ons straatbeeld. Zeker kleine verbouwingen moeten zonder toetsing kunnen.

207002.explanation: Maar dit is ook al in hoge mate het geval in Groningen stad.

207003.explanation: Iedereen heeft zeggenschap over eigen lijf en tijd. Dit is de definitie van uitbuiting. Wij zijn pertinent tegen dwang.

207008.explanation: Gemeenschapsgeld mag niet misbruikt worden, daarom zijn wij voor.

207009.explanation: Er gelden al meer dan genoeg beperkingen voor het autoverkeer. Maak rijden in Groningen niet onmogelijk.

207010.explanation: Groningen is een groeiende stad, achter de vismarkt is de parkeergarage door de gemeente gesloten. We komen nu juist parkeerplekken tekort!

207014.explanation: Er gelden al meer dan genoeg beperkingen voor het autoverkeer. Maak rijden in Groningen niet onmogelijk.

207016.explanation: Wij zijn voor, zo lang het initiatief vanuit burgers zelf komt. De gemeente heeft slechts als taak om, als daar vraag naar is, faciliterend op te treden. Bureaucratie en kosten voor de gemeenschap moeten tot het absolute minimum beperkt worden.

206950.explanation: Gas extraction has caused a disaster in Groningen. Many residents are still waiting for safe homes, resolved damage claims, and genuine recognition. As a government, we must take every step that contributes to damage recovery. Municipal assistance makes it more accessible.

206952.explanation: Students are exploited by slum landlords and, due to performance pressure, are more likely to experience mental health issues during their studies. A student help desk can help young people with questions about rental agreements, their rights, and where to go for help with mental health issues.

206954.explanation: Art and culture is allowed to be uncomfortable, can change your perspective, and should be diverse. Subsidies should therefore also go to small-scale creators, not just heritage. We want to increase the funding for art and culture.

206957.explanation: We want to invest in both the climate and healthcare. The last four years we showed that that’s possible. For example, by maintaining the property tax, we can invest in both.

206958.explanation: Everything indicates we've already surpassed the 1.5 degrees of global warming. We can turn the tide if we make different choices. We want to cut all ties with fossil fuel companies and are working on a ban on fossil fuel advertising.

206959.explanation: This tax is relatively low, and we use it to create a pleasant and green living environment. This is a major benefit for businesses and residents.

206960.explanation: Advertising encourages overconsumption. We want to ban advertising in public spaces, with the exception of government campaigns, cultural events, and charities. Until then, advertising will remain taxed.

206965.explanation: No human being is illegal! Everyone has the right to humane shelter, regardless of residency status or papers. We maintain the bed-bath-bread arrangement.

206966.explanation: Companies in the Netherlands make millions by exploiting migrant workers. If they lose their jobs, they often lose their homes as well. By separating their housing and work, they are better protected. Municipalities must protect vulnerable groups from criminal landlords and employers.

206967.explanation: Everyone has the right to a decent life, regardless of their origin or residence status. A Stadjerspas gives them access to things like sports, culture, and veterinary care. This not only helps prevent poverty but also ensures they can participate in Groningen from day one.

206973.explanation: We want to have full control over ''Warmtestad''. This way, we can decide what happens with the revenues from those connections and ensure that the income flows back to our residents. In this way we invest in the heating network, sustainable heat sources, and local neighborhood heating.

206975.explanation: To tackle the nature crisis and preserve habitats for animals living in the wild: forests are needed. In addition trees prevent overheated cities and enhance biodiversity. The more trees, the better. The ''MEER bos'' is a wonderful project by residents that we want to bring to life.

206976.explanation: No disruptive activities, such as events, are planned in or near nature reserves. For each type of event outside natural areas, the effects on local residents must be considered. A livable Groningen for both people and animals is important to our considerations.

206980.explanation: We are expanding accessible drug testing to ensure safe drug use. At parties where hard drugs will likely be used we will deploy staff to provide information and, where necessary, support.

206981.explanation: We want all children to have access to sports and grow up healthy. Free membership can make the decision to participate in sports much easier, especially for children from low-income families. We do believe that parents with higher incomes can contribute to their children's sports clubs.

206986.explanation: Arts and culture are very important in any democracy. They encourage reflection, stir emotions, move and disrupt. Children must be able to develop their social-, emotional-, motor- and creative skills. Culture contributes to those things.

206987.explanation: FC Groningen is very important to many residents in Groningen. We want to support this process, but a substantial financial contribution is not feasible. The cost-benefit analysis, along with the contributions and vision of supporters and the club will be decisive factors in the decision.

206988.explanation: We have the best cultural offering in the north of the Netherlands. By investing in the renovation of De Oosterpoort and the construction of De Grote Popzaal, we are ensuring high-quality culture in the city for future generations.

206989.explanation: Violence isn't the solution. Furthermore institutional racism and ethnic profiling are a problem within the police and law enforcement. Groningen residents with a migration background may face disproportionate violence more often if enforcement officers are armed.

206993.explanation: Housing must be distributed fairly. Everyone should have equal opportunities. In villages it can be helpful to give young people the option to stay in their village. Housing associations in cities already have a system of appropriate allocation.

206994.explanation: There's a shortage of social housing. Everyone who is entitled to social housing should have equal opportunities. That's what the Party for the Animals stands for. We do, however, support giving priority to people in a vulnerable situation.

206996.explanation: Housing is a right, and should not be a profit model. We will maintain the owner-occupancy requirement and combat vacancy. We believe the municipality should immediately implement anti-speculation measures wherever possible to keep investors out.

206998.explanation: We want to build homes for the coming decades, not just the next few years. Building regulations help prevent poor-quality homes from being built or prevent the built of homes that don't fit well into their surroundings. Homes must be sustainable, healthy, and attractive.

207002.explanation: Social housing should be distributed across all neighborhoods and villages. We strive for a community where people connect, regardless of income. This must be done in a nature-inclusive way. Green spaces must be preserved.

207003.explanation: Groningen needs to shift its focus from making profit to ensure well-being. Social assistance is an important safety net and forcing others to do something in return actually increases inequality and stress. The municipality should primarily help people.

207008.explanation: As far as the Party for the Animals is concerned, there will be no hotline for benefit fraud in Groningen. The existing hotline will be shut down. Trust in our citizens must be paramount in the government. We don't want situations like the Dutch childcare benefits scandal.

207009.explanation: We discourage polluting traffic and we promote clean traffic. We give pedestrians and cyclists more space. We contribute to climate goals, reclaim green spaces in our neighbourhoods and make outdoor areas safer for children and animals.

207010.explanation: As far as we're concerned, no new parking spaces will be added. The municipality will eliminate some parking spaces and convert them into wider sidewalks, children's play areas, flower-filled verges, small parks, or bicycle parking spaces.

207014.explanation: Motorized traffic within built-up areas should have a speed limit of 30 km/h. Road layouts will be adapted where possible so we can create space for greenery and children can play in the streets.

207016.explanation: Every village, neighborhood, and district should have a food garden and a community center where people can meet and eat together. This way we bring people closer together, reduce health disparities and increase equal opportunities

statements.title: The municipality must reduce the number of parking spaces.
The municipality should not do business with companies that use fossil fuels.
The municipality must urge the national government to establish a single municipal team that manages everything relating to earthquake damage.
The majority of the subsidies for culture should go towards heritage and local traditions.
All refugees in the municipality must receive a Stadjerspas. This is a pass specifically for people with little money.
The municipality must allow evening events to continue until later.
The municipality should spend the money for climate policy on care plans.
In nightlife and entertainment areas, there should be multiple places where people can have their drugs tested to ensure they are safe.
The municipality must help supporters of FC Groningen to become half-owners of the football club, instead of commercial investors.
The municipality must abolish the advertising tax. This includes taxes for facade advertisements, signs, illuminated advertising and the like.
The municipality must pay for a sports club membership for all children under the age of 16 years.
Teachers, nurses and police officers should be given priority for public-sector rented homes.
Employers must ensure that migrant workers live at or near their workplace.
Groningen must distribute public-sector rented homes throughout the municipality.
The municipality should establish a student affairs department that also employs students.
Groningen must sell the municipal heating company WarmteStad.
Municipal law enforcement officers (BOAs) may carry a baton.
The maximum speed should be 30 km/h everywhere, except on the ring road and a few main roads.
Half of the rental homes in new construction must go to people who have a connection to Groningen.
The municipality must have at least 60 hectares of new forest planted.
The municipality must abolish the rules concerning the external appearance of buildings for the construction and renovation of homes.
The municipality should have a large concert hall built on the south side of the station.
The option to report benefit fraud through the municipality's website should remain in place.
Anyone who buys a house in our municipality must live there themselves.
The municipality must reduce the tax on terraces.
The municipality must stop accommodating people who do not have a valid residence permit.
There should be more streets in which the priority of cars is reduced.
People who receive social assistance benefits must do socially meaningful work in return.
The municipality should spend more money on introducing children to art and culture.
Every village and every neighbourhood should have its own food garden.
